facts: Venus reaches temperatures of 462°C, hot enough to melt lead
Its surface pressure is 92 times that of Earth, comparable to being almost a mile underwater
The planet's atmosphere is dense enough to trap heat, making it hotter than Mercury despite being farther from the Sun
idea.payoff: and we still don't know why it's so much hotter than Mercury
idea.title: Venus: Scorching Hot 🌡
idea.description: Imagine a place where the surface temperature can melt lead 🌡️. Welcome to Venus, the hottest planet in our solar system, with a scorching atmosphere that's still shrouded in mystery 🤔. Will we ever uncover the secrets behind its extreme heat?

scenes.visual: A dark space background with a fiery red planet outline (Venus), a large question mark appears and slightly pulsates.
Zoom into a vivid orange/red depiction of Venus's surface. A small icon of a melting lead bar appears next to the text.
Venus appears, then a visual overlay representing immense pressure, like deep ocean depths. Text updates with a small graphic illustrating '1 Mile Underwater'.
Venus with thick, swirling atmospheric layers. A simplified graphic appears comparing Venus and Mercury's positions relative to the Sun, highlighting Venus is hotter despite being farther.
Venus is shown shrouded in mystery, perhaps with faint question marks or a swirling nebula effect around it, text pulsates slowly to emphasize the unknown.
